While going home to lunch,
Charles Carter, a 9-year-old and a
ward of the Washington Children's
Home society, was struck by an en-
CINE in the Great Northern yards
and was removed to the home in a
dying condition.


Mrs. n. L. Moody, 626 Cedar
street, has postponed the reception
to be given at her home Saturday
night on account of the death of
her father, H. w. Foster, of Lin
coin, III.


A former resident Of Spokane,
but now of Los Angeles, Cal, G. A..
Brock, a jeweler, is now on visit
to this city.


A suit for $500 damages for in.
juries received while clearing a
wreck, was brought against the
Great Northern Railway company
by Carl A. Ruiagroff yesterday.


MISLEADING the proprietor for a
burglar, a call was sent in from
the Washington Wagon & Carriage
Co.'S store, 1112 First avenue, and
when the police arrived, they
found the owner taking a nap in a
hammock slung in the rear of the
store.


Robert RiddeII and Howard
Douglass of Calgary, Alberta, are
in Spokane on their way to Black
foot, Mont, to take over the buff
falo herd purchased by the Do
minion government from Michael
Pablo few years ago.


The city council has decided to
purchase $4,000 in city warrants,
which are drawing per cent in
terest.


Friends are seeking to learn
the whereabouts of DR.. l. N Robb,
formerly of Newark, Ohio. DR..
Robb came west a few years ago
and has not been heard of since.
e. Kennedy was sentenced to
serve ten days in Jail by Justice
S. c. Hyde Monday for. an attempt
ed assault on Marie Paul, May Is.
